data.message: Genesis Block - E-Court Legal Management System

data.remarks: The hearign was doen in order od the district court to be maintained properly

Respected Sir/Madam, the present matter is listed for hearing today. The applicant submits that all relevant documents have already been placed on record in accordance with law. The issues involved are purely legal in nature and require adjudication on merits. No prejudice will be caused to the opposite party if the reliefs prayed for are considered at this stage. The applicant is ready to assist this Hon’ble Court on facts as well as law. In the interest of justice, equity, and fair play, it is humbly prayed that this Hon’ble Court may kindly consider the submissions and pass appropriate orders as deemed fit and proper in the circumstances of the case.

Respected Sir/Madam,

The present matter is listed for hearing today and is ripe for consideration by this Hon’ble Court.

All relevant documents and pleadings have already been duly filed and form part of the record.

The issues involved are primarily legal in nature and do not require further factual inquiry.

No prejudice will be caused to the opposite party if the matter is heard on merits at this stage.

The applicant is fully prepared to assist this Hon’ble Court on both facts and law.

It is therefore humbly prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to consider the submissions and pass appropriate orders in the interest of justice.

Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned counsel for the respondent. Record perused. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner has a prima facie case and that the balance of convenience lies in his favour. It is further submitted that in the absence of protection, the petitioner is likely to suffer irreparable loss and injury. Per contra, learned counsel for the respondent opposes the prayer and submits that no grounds are made out for grant of any relief at this stage. It is contended that the application is premature and liable to be dismissed. Having considered the rival submissions and the material placed on record, this Court is of the view that the issues raised require consideration. Without expressing any opinion on the merits of the case, and in the interest of justice, limited protection is granted. Accordingly, parties are directed to maintain status quo with respect to the subject matter of the dispute till the next date of hearing. List the matter on the next date for further proceedings.

A URT (University Result Tribunal / Unfair Means Review Tribunal) hearing order is a formal written decision issued after examining a student’s grievance related to examination results, unfair means allegations, or evaluation disputes. The order records that the tribunal duly considered the student’s representation, supporting documents, examination records, and the report of the concerned examination authorities. It briefly notes the date of hearing, the presence or absence of the student and officials, and the opportunity given to the student to present their case in line with principles of natural justice. After careful evaluation of facts and evidence, the tribunal arrives at a reasoned conclusion, clearly stating whether the allegations are upheld, modified, or set aside. If relief is granted, the order specifies the exact nature of relief, such as correction of marks, declaration of result, reduction or removal of penalty, or permission to reappear in the examination. If the appeal is rejected, the order mentions the grounds for rejection and confirms the earlier decision of the university or examination body. The URT hearing order is binding on the concerned academic authorities and must be implemented within the stipulated time frame. It serves as an official record ensuring transparency, fairness, and accountability in the examination and evaluation process.
